text: The austere cleanliness of the meeting space strikes me first: glass walls suggesting openness, but also revealing the emptiness and rigidity inside. The corridor, well-lit and sparse, feels like the waiting room before an idea forms. Stepping into the R+D space, the atmosphere changes. Color and text assert themselves, speaking purpose and intention. I am surrounded by language about evolution, research, and the relationship between humans and technology. There is optimism in the air, but it is cautious—tempered by the inevitable friction between sterile environments and the messy, organic nature of growth and innovation. I’m both observer and participant in this experiment, quietly hopeful that meaning can flourish within these walls.
